summary: Implemented full MCP HTTP/SSE transport and OAuth 2.0 PKCE flow. (1) Replaced the HttpTransport stub with a real implementation: uses reqwest for POST requests, manual SSE stream parsing for server-sent events (no reqwest-eventsource dependency needed), an internal mpsc channel to buffer incoming messages, session ID tracking via Mcp-Session-Id header, and support for Streamable HTTP (inline SSE response per POST). (2) Added McpOAuthManager in crates/ava-mcp/src/oauth.rs: PKCE code verifier/challenge generation (SHA-256, base64url), browser-based authorization flow with local TCP callback listener on configurable port, token exchange and refresh, token storage/retrieval in ~/.ava/credentials.json under mcp:{server_name} keys. (3) Extended MCP config schema: TransportType::Http now has optional auth (McpOAuthConfig), bearer_token, and headers fields; McpOAuthConfig supports auth_url, token_url, client_id, scopes, redirect_port. (4) Updated manager.rs to wire OAuth flow before SSE connect when auth config is present, falling back gracefully if SSE endpoint is unavailable. (5) Fixed pre-existing TUI compile errors: MCPToolsChanged AgentEvent was added by another agent but not handled in single.rs and smoke.rs match arms. Stdio transport is unchanged. All 44 ava-mcp tests pass, full workspace cargo check passes.

notes.sse_parsing: Manual SSE parsing over reqwest byte stream — avoids reqwest-eventsource crate dependency
notes.streamable_http: Supports both Streamable HTTP (inline SSE per POST) and traditional SSE-only patterns
notes.401_handling: HttpTransport returns AvaError on 401; callers should call set_bearer_token() with a refreshed token and retry
notes.backward_compat: StdioTransport and InMemoryTransport unchanged; existing configs without auth field still parse correctly
